title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Eastchester, NY | $900 - $2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Eastchester, Westchester. Humane, safe bat control for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Dealing with bats in your Eastchester home or business requires immediate professional attention to protect your property and health. Best Pest Control NYC provides comprehensive bat removal and exclusion services throughout Eastchester, using humane methods that comply with New York wildlife regulations. Our experienced technicians understand the unique challenges that Eastchester properties face and deliver effective, long-lasting solutions.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has been serving Eastchester residents for years with specialized bat control expertise, offering same-day emergency services and guaranteed exclusion work. Our licensed wildlife technicians use proven techniques to safely remove bats and seal entry points, ensuring they won't return to your property.
localContext: Eastchester's mix of historic Colonial homes near the Village Green and mid-century properties around Lake Isle creates ideal environments for bat colonization, especially in older attics and chimneys. The tree-lined neighborhoods and proximity to conservation areas mean bats frequently find their way into residential structures seeking shelter.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Eastchester?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Eastchester typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and extent of exclusion work needed. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in Eastchester, NY?
faqs.answer: Yes, but bat removal must follow New York State regulations. Bats cannot be excluded during maternity season (roughly May through August) when young are present. Our licensed professionals ensure all removal work complies with state and local wildlife laws while using humane exclusion methods.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my Eastchester home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, dark grease stains near entry points, accumulation of guano (droppings) in attics or around the exterior, and seeing bats emerge from your home at sunset. If you notice any of these signs, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: What does the bat exclusion process involve?
faqs.answer: Our bat exclusion process includes a thorough inspection to identify entry points and roosting areas, installation of one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return, sealing all potential entry points with durable materials, attic cleanup and sanitization if needed, and a warranty on our exclusion work to ensure bats don't return.
